symptoms.symptom_name: Nocturnal wheeze, chest tightness and orthopnoea

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Nocturnal wheeze/tightness with a known asthma phenotype is typical.
Orthopnoea can be cardiac; requires in-person assessment.
Reliever refractoriness marks a significant exacerbation needing urgent assessment.
Absence of life-threatening markers indicates a moderate (not yet life-threatening) exacerbation, but reliever failure still warrants urgent assessment.

symptoms.symptom_name: Adult-onset asthma, nasal polyps, eosinophilia (EGPA prodrome)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Adult-onset asthma + polyps + eosinophilia is the classic EGPA prodrome, warranting work-up for systemic vasculitic involvement.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent in-person respiratory assessment (auscultation, peak flow, oximetry, nebuliser response)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assess severity and treat — the key steps telehealth cannot provide for a reliever-refractory attack.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: EGPA work-up (eosinophil count, ANCA, ECG/troponin, urinalysis, nerve conduction)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Screen for eosinophilic/vasculitic organ involvement once the acute attack is managed.
